role: Member of Guardian Council; substitute Friday Prayer Imam of Tehran (2005–present)
bio: Ahmad Khatami is a hardline Shia cleric and member of the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) and the [Assembly of Experts](/orgs/assembly-of-experts). He shares a surname with reformist President [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami) but is entirely unrelated — a fact that gave him national prominence in 1997. He was appointed substitute Friday Prayer Imam of Tehran in 2005 by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei), becoming the youngest person to hold that position. During the [2009 Green Movement protests](/events/green-movement-protests-2009) he declared the protesters to be engaging in *mohareb* (war against God), a capital offence, and called on the judiciary to execute opposition leaders [Mir-Hossein Mousavi](/people/mir-hossein-mousavi) and [Mehdi Karroubi](/people/mehdi-karroubi) as 'leaders of sedition'. Appointed to the [Guardian Council](/orgs/guardian-council) by [Ali Khamenei](/people/ali-khamenei) in 2020 to replace Mohammad Yazdi. He was mentioned as a candidate for Supreme Leader succession but reportedly opposed [Mojtaba Khamenei](/people/mojtaba-khamenei)'s elevation in the Assembly of Experts.
editorial_notes: Born 8 May 1960 in Semnan. Not related to President Mohammad Khatami. Guardian Council member since 2020. Sanctioned by the US and EU for inciting violence against protesters.
